title: artemisia alba subsp. saxatilis
intro: Artemisia alba subsp. saxatilis, also known as rock sage, is a subspecies of the white wormwood plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Artemisia alba subsp. saxatilis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and improve mood.
